{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Antigua and Barbuda women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Antigua and Barbuda
| Badge                 = Antigua and Barbuda Football.png
| Badge_size            = 180px
| Nickname              = ''The Benna Boys''
| Association           = [[Antigua and Barbuda Football Association]] (ABFA)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] (Caribbean)
| Confederation         = [[CONCACAF]] (North America)
| Coach                 = [[Jacques Passy]]<ref>https://inside.fifa.com/associations/ATG</ref>
| Captain               = [[Quinton Griffith]]
| Most caps             = [[Peter Byers (footballer)|Peter Byers]] (91)
| Top scorer            = [[Peter Byers (footballer)|Peter Byers]] (44)
| Home Stadium          = [[Antigua Recreation Ground]]<br>[[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = ATG
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|ATG}}
| FIFA max              = 70
| FIFA max date         = October 2014
| FIFA min              = 170
| FIFA min date         = December 2003 – January 2004
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Antigua and Barbuda}}
| Elo max               = 131
| Elo max date          = November 2010
| Elo min               = 179
| Elo min date          = November 2007
| pattern_la1           = _jomasupernova19by
| pattern_b1            = _a&b_bennaboys2021h
| pattern_ra1           = _home_jomasupernova19by
| pattern_sh1           = _a&b_bennaboys2021
| pattern_so1           = 
| leftarm1              = 000000
| body1                 = 000000
| rightarm1             = 000000
| shorts1               = FFFF00
| socks1                = FFFF00
| pattern_la2           = _jomasupernova19antigua
| pattern_b2            = _a&b bennaboys2021away
| pattern_ra2           = _away_jomasupernova19by
| pattern_sh2           = _antigua&barbuda2021away
| pattern_so2           = 
| leftarm2              = DF0101
| body2                 = DF0101
| rightarm2             = DF0101
| shorts2               = 000000
| socks2                = 000000
| First game            = {{flagicon|British Leeward Islands|1871}} '''Antigua and Barbuda''' 2–0 {{fb|MSR}}<br>([[Basseterre]], Saint Christopher and Nevis; 1 February 1950)
| Largest win           = {{fb|ATG}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|VIR}}<br>([[North Sound]], Antigua and Barbuda; 11 October 2011)
| Largest loss          = {{fb|TRI}} 11–1 {{fb-rt|ATG}}<br>([[Port of Spain]], Trinidad and Tobago; 10 November 1972)
| Regional name         = 
| Regional cup apps     = 
| Regional cup first    = 
| Regional cup best     = 
| 2ndRegional name      = [[CFU Championship]] / [[Caribbean Cup]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 11
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[1978 CFU Championship|1978]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Runners-up ([[1988 CFU Championship|1988]])
| medaltemplates        = 
{{MedalCompetition|[[CFU Championship]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1988 CFU Championship|1988 Martinique]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Antigua and Barbuda national football team''' represents [[Antigua and Barbuda]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], which is governed by the [[Antigua and Barbuda Football Association]] founded in 1928.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-03-07 |title=ANTIGUA AND BARBUDA |url=https://www.concacaf.com/inside-concacaf/member-associations/antigua-and-barbuda/ |access-date=2024-04-16 |website=Concacaf |language=en}}</ref> It has been a member of [[FIFA]] and [[CONCACAF]] since 1972. Regionally, it is a member of [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] in the Caribbean Zone.

Antigua and Barbuda has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]] and the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]], but has participated four times in the League B of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]]. Regionally, the team finished as runners-up in the [[CFU Championship]] in [[1988 CFU Championship|1988]].

Antigua and Barbuda's debut in international competitions was in the [[1973 CONCACAF Championship qualification]], which also served as the CONCACAF qualifiers for the [[1974 FIFA World Cup]]. The team achieved its first victory in 1950, defeating [[Montserrat national football team|Montserrat]] 2–0 in the [[Leeward Islands Tournament]], which was also the first match in the team's history.

==History==
===First official international match===
On November 10, 1972, Antigua and Barbuda's first official match was in the 1973 CONCACAF Championship qualification, losing to [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] 11–1 in Port of Spain, which is the worst defeat in the team's history.

===Friendlies against European teams===
On 17 December 2005, the Antigua and Barbuda national team played their first match against a European opponent after the [[Hungarian Football Federation]] invited "the Benna Boys" to play [[Hungary national football team|Hungary]] in an international [[Exhibition game|friendly]] at [[Lockhart Stadium]] in [[Fort Lauderdale, Florida|Fort Lauderdale]], Florida. This was the first match coached by former national striker [[Derrick Edwards]]. Hungary defeated Antigua and Barbuda 3–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.caribbeannewsnow.com/caribnet/2005/12/21/beat.shtml|title=Caribbean Net News: Hungary beat Antigua-Barbuda in exhibition match|work=caribbeannewsnow.com|access-date=4 January 2017|archive-date=17 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160417060543/http://www.caribbeannewsnow.com/caribnet/2005/12/21/beat.shtml|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 2 May 2016, the [[Estonian Football Association]] announced that [[Estonia national football team|their national team]] would play Antigua and Barbuda in an international friendly on 22 November. It was the team's second match against a European opponent. Estonia won the game 1–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.jalgpall.ee/news.php?st=style_fp.css&news_id=10130|title=Eesti Jalgpalli Liit|work=jalgpall.ee|access-date=4 January 2017|archive-date=4 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160604064501/http://www.jalgpall.ee/news.php?st=style_fp.css&news_id=10130|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2018 drug bust in Jamaica===
During the March 2018 [[FIFA International Match Calendar|FIFA Calendar]] Antigua and Barbuda played Jamaica, drawing 1–1 away at [[Sabina Park]], levelling in the last seconds of match from a header from [[Peter Byers (footballer)|Peter Byers]]. Upon the team's preparation for their departure back to Antigua, assistant coach Derrick Edwards and equipment manager Danny Benjamin were arrested and charged with possession of, dealing in, and taking steps to export [[Cannabis (drug)|marijuana]], and conspiracy at the [[Norman Manley International Airport]]. Initially, Edwards was awarded [[bail]] and Benjamin was held without bail; after a month, both Edwards and Benjamin were placed under [[house arrest]] and [[curfew]] and gave up their travel documents.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Helps |first=HG |date=1 April 2018 |title=A coach's ganja plan |url=https://www.jamaicaobserver.com/front-page/a-coach-8217-s-ganja-pain-was-derrick-8216-pretty-boy-8217-edwards-set-up-with-drug-found-in-his-luggage-_129355 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180402094803/https://www.jamaicaobserver.com/front-page/a-coach-8217-s-ganja-pain-was-derrick-8216-pretty-boy-8217-edwards-set-up-with-drug-found-in-his-luggage-_129355 |archive-date=2 April 2018 |access-date=17 August 2022 |website=[[Jamaica Observer]]}}</ref> Both men were acquitted of the crime on 14 June, citing there was not enough evidence to prove their involvement in the carrying the drugs with the intention of smuggling them out of Jamaica.{{Cn|date=August 2022}}

==Team image==

===Nicknames===
The official nickname of the Antigua and Barbuda national football team is "the Benna Boys", referring to a genre of Antiguan and Barbudan music similar to calypso. The name was selected in a contest with the fanbase in 2012 after the team advanced to the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF third round|third round]] of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2014 World Cup qualifiers]]. "The Benna Boys" beat out other entries in the contest such as "Beach Boys", "Iron Bandits", "Rhythm Warriors" and "Party Crashers".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://antiguafootball.com/text-to-774-3307-your-choice-for-the-name-the-team-contest/|title=Text to 774 – 3307 your choice for the name the team contest|accessdate=28 March 2023|archive-date=28 May 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160528161126/http://antiguafootball.com/text-to-774-3307-your-choice-for-the-name-the-team-contest/|url-status=live}}</ref> In the past, the national team was unofficially known as "the Wadadli Boys", another name that was in the contest but did not win.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.topendsports.com/sport/soccer/team-nicknames.htm|title=Football (Soccer) Team Nicknames|website=www.topendsports.com|accessdate=28 March 2023|archive-date=28 July 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170728235633/http://www.topendsports.com/sport/soccer/team-nicknames.htm|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Kit suppliers===
{{Commons|Antigua and Barbuda national football team kits}}
The kit of the national team has been manufactured by Spanish-based [[Joma]] since March 2021, ahead of the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF first round|first round]] of the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2022 World Cup qualifiers]].{{Cn|date=August 2022}}
{| class="wikitable"
|- style="color:black;"
! style="width:150px; background:#FCD116;" |Kit manufacturer
! style="width:120px; background:#FCD116;" |Time period
|-
| [[Admiral Sportswear|Admiral]]
| 1995–1999
|-
| {{ill|Virma|it}}
| 2000–2003
|-
| [[Adidas]]
| 2006–2011
|-
| [[Peak Sport Products|Peak]]
| 2012–2016
|-
| [[Admiral Sportswear|Admiral]]
| 2016–2020
|-
| [[Joma]]
| 2021–present
|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Antigua and Barbuda national football team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group A|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 6 June
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ATG}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|CUB}}
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017741
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Pedro Bravo (Cuban footballer)|Pedro Bravo]] {{goal|45+8}}
|location   = [[Piggotts]], [[Antigua and Barbuda]]
|stadium    = [[ABFA Technical Centre]]
|attendance = 428
|referee    = Kwinsi Williams ([[Trinidad and Tobago Football Association|Trinidad and Tobago]])
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group A|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 June
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-6}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|HON|2022}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|ATG}}
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017739 Report (FIFA)]<br/>[https://www.concacaf.com/en/world-cup-qualifying-men/game-details/?competition=3a0j0giz3c3ajw9h59evv7lqt&season=excflxj3ypj69gf2wlx7kpudw&match=52kn7ororn34udib8yyrdht04 Report (CONCACAF)]
|goals1     = 
*[[Getsel Montes|Montes]] {{goal|49}}
*[[Alexy Vega|Vega]] {{goal|80}}
|goals2     =
|location   = 
|stadium    = [[Estadio Nacional Chelato Uclés]], [[Tegucigalpa]]
|attendance = 7,905
|referee    = Waldir García ([[Salvadoran Football Federation|El Salvador]])
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 12 November 
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ATG}}
|score      = 0–0
|team2      = {{fb|ARU}}
|report     = https://abstvradio.com/benna-boys-set-for-international-friendlies-next-month/
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = [[North Sound]], [[Antigua and Barbuda]]
|stadium    = [[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = D
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 18 November 
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ATG}}
|score      = 1–4  
|team2      = {{fb|GUY}}
|report     = https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c3rj0elq2n7t
|goals1     =
*[[Quinton Griffith|Griffith]] {{goal|61|pen.}}
|goals2     =
*[[Omari Glasgow|Glasgow]] {{goal|12||23|pen.}}
*[[Osaze De Rosario|De Rosario]] {{goal|71}}
*[[Stephen Duke-McKenna|Duke-McKenna]] {{goal|76}}
|location   = [[North Sound]], [[Antigua and Barbuda]]
|stadium    = [[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Andrew Samuel ([[Trinidad and Tobago Football Association|Trinidad and Tobago]])
|result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==

===Current coaching staff===
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! style="width:200px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Name
! style="width:200px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Position
|-
|{{flagicon|MEX}} [[Jacques Passy]]
|Head coach
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Janiel Simon]]
|Manager
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Lenny Hewlett]]
|Assistant coach
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Schyan Jeffers]]
|Assistant coach
|-
|{{flagicon|ESP}} [[Eduardo Suarez]]
|Assistant coach
|-
|{{flagicon|ESP}} [[Juan Lecona Nava]]
|Goalkeeper coach
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[McClean Lawrence]]
|Physiotherapist
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Danny Benjamin]]
|Equipment manager
|}

===Manager history===
[[File:Piotr Nowak in 2016.jpg|thumb|upright|Polish manager [[Piotr Nowak]] was at the helm of Antigua and Barbuda when they achieved their highest FIFA ranking (70) in October 2014.]]

The following is a list of head coaches of the Antigua and Barbuda national team throughout the years.{{Cn|date=August 2022}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
! style="width:200px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Name
! style="width:100px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Period
! style="width:30px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Matches
! style="width:30px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" | Wins
! style="width:30px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Draws
! style="width:30px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Losses
! style="width:200px; background:#FCD116; color:black;" |Notes
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Rudi Gutendorf]]
| style="text-align: left;" |1976
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|SCG}} [[Zoran Vraneš]]
| style="text-align: left;" |1998–2000
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |Fourth place at the [[1998 Caribbean Cup]]
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Walter Gama]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2001–2002
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Rolston Williams]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2004
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Derrick Edwards]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2005–2008
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|SCO}} [[Willie Donachie]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2008
| style="text-align: left;" |16
| style="text-align: left;" |8
| style="text-align: left;" |3
| style="text-align: left;" |5
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Rowan Benjamin]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2008–2011
| style="text-align: left;" |11
| style="text-align: left;" |4
| style="text-align: left;" |3
| style="text-align: left;" |4
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Tom Curtis (footballer)|Tom Curtis]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2011–2012
| style="text-align: left;" |21
| style="text-align: left;" |8
| style="text-align: left;" |3
| style="text-align: left;" |10
| style="text-align: left;" |Semifinal round of the [[2014 World Cup Qualifiers]]
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Rolston Williams]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2012–2014
| style="text-align: left;" |6
| style="text-align: left;" |4
| style="text-align: left;" |0
| style="text-align: left;" |2
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|POL}} [[Piotr Nowak]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2014–2015
| style="text-align: left;" |12
| style="text-align: left;" |5
| style="text-align: left;" |2
| style="text-align: left;" |5
| style="text-align: left;" |Highest [[FIFA Ranking]] (70) under his tenure
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Rolston Williams]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2015–2018
| style="text-align: left;" |8
| style="text-align: left;" |3
| style="text-align: left;" |0
| style="text-align: left;" |5
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[Derrick Edwards]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2018–2019
| style="text-align: left;" |6
| style="text-align: left;" |2
| style="text-align: left;" |1
| style="text-align: left;" |3
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" |{{flagicon|DRC}} [[Michel Mazingu-Dinzey|Michél Dinzey]]
| style="text-align: left;" |2019–2020
| style="text-align: left;" |10
| style="text-align: left;" |4
| style="text-align: left;" |0
| style="text-align: left;" |6
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" | {{flagicon|ATG}} [[Mikele Leigertwood]] 
| style="text-align: left;" |2021–2025
| style="text-align: left;" |25
| style="text-align: left;" |6
| style="text-align: left;" |4
| style="text-align: left;" |15
| style="text-align: left;" |
|-
| style="text-align: left;" | {{flagicon|MEX}} [[Jacques Passy]] 
| style="text-align: left;" |2025-
| style="text-align: left;" |4
| style="text-align: left;" |0
| style="text-align: left;" |1
| style="text-align: left;" |3
| style="text-align: left;" |
|}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]] matches against [[Aruba national football team|Aruba]] and [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] on 12 and 18 November 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Here’s the final Senior Men’s National Team set to represent Antigua and Barbuda in the Concacaf Series |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo?fbid=1395295472598334&set=a.635134335281122&locale=en_GB |website=Facebook |publisher=ABFA - Antigua And Barbuda Football Association |date=11 November 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 12 November 2025, after the match against [[Aruba national football team|Aruba]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Jayden Martin]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2002|11|7}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=GK|name=[[Zaieem Scott]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|07|10|df=yes}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Future Pro Academy]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=GK|name=[[Shahoi Dorsett]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2004|6|23}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[CF Lorca Deportiva]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Zafique Drew]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2003|8|30}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Kylano Isaac]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|01|10}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Chojniczanka Chojnice]]|clubnat=POL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=DF|name=[[Tyrik Hughes]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2005|7|13}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[CF Lorca Deportiva]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Amir Daley]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|02|26}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Birmingham Legion FC|Birmingham Legion]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Jahzinho O'Garro]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2002|12|12}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=DF|name=[[Leroy Graham]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|12|07}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Sean Tomlinson]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2004|3|25}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[CF Lorca Deportiva]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=MF|name=[[Dion Pereira]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1999|3|25}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Crawley Town F.C.|Crawley Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[TJ Bramble]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2001|5|9}}|caps=21|goals=2|club=[[Maidstone United F.C.|Maidstone United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=MF|name=[[Quinton Griffith]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1992|2|27}}|caps=89|goals=11|club=[[Grenades F.C.|Grenades]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=MF|name=[[Shalon Knight]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2000|3|4}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Dannen Francis]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2004|12|14}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[CD Almuñécar City]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Raheem Deterville]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1999|12|1}}|caps=22|goals=2|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=FW|name=[[Daryl Massicot]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2005|2|12}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Cavalier F.C.|Cavalier]]|clubnat=JAM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[D'Andre Bishop]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2002|10|2}}|caps=29|goals=3|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=FW|name=[[Karique Knight]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|04|03|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Antigua and Barbuda squad within the last twelve months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Taj Moore]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2005|6|16}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Brock Badgers]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|HON|2022}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Luther James-Wildin]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1997|12|3}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Stevenage F.C.|Stevenage]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|HON|2022}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Antonio Morgan]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2006|3|29}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Lewes F.C.|Lewes]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|HON|2022}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Drake Hadeed]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2007|11|21}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Antequera CF|Antequera]]|clubnat=ESP|latest=v. {{fb|HON|2022}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{updated|18 November 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Antigua and Barbuda |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/8/Antigua_And_Barbuda.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Antigua and Barbuda.''

[[File:Peter-Byers-09-05-27.jpg|thumb|150px|[[Peter Byers (footballer)|Peter Byers]] is Antigua and Barbuda's top goalscorer and most-capped player.]]

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Rank
!width=175px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Player
!width=50px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;|Caps
!width=50px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;|Goals
!width=100px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Peter Byers (footballer)|Peter Byers]] || 91 || 44 || 2004–2021
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Quinton Griffith]]''' || 89 || 11 || 2009–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[George Dublin]] || 67 || 4 || 2000–2012
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Tamorley Thomas]] || 64 || 12 || 2002–2021
|-
| 5 ||align=left| [[Karanja Mack]] || 58 || 0 || 2006–2022
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Ranjae Christian]] || 54 || 5 || 1998–2012
|-
| rowspan=2|7 ||align=left| [[Randolph Burton]] || 52 || 15 || 2008–2016
|-
|align=left| [[Akeem Thomas]] || 52 || 2 || 2008–2018
|-
| rowspan=2|9 ||align=left| [[Gayson Gregory]] || 49 || 8 || 2000–2014
|-
|align=left| [[Molvin James]] || 49 || 0 || 2007–2021
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Rank
!width=175px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Player
!width=50px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Goals
!width=50px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Caps
!width=50px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Ratio
!width=100px; style=background:#FCD116; color:black;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Peter Byers (footballer)|Peter Byers]] || 44 || 91 || {{#expr:44/91 round 2}} || 2004–2021
|-
| 2 ||align=left| [[Derrick Edwards]] || 18 || 22 || {{#expr:18/22 round 2}} || {{nowrap|1988–2000}}
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[Randolph Burton]] || 15 || 52 || {{#expr:15/52 round 2}} || 2008–2016
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Tamorley Thomas]] || 12 || 64 || {{#expr:12/64 round 2}} || 2002–2021
|-
| 5 ||align=left| '''[[Quinton Griffith]]''' || 11 || 89 || {{#expr:10/87 round 2}} || 2009–present
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Jamie Thomas (footballer, born 1985)|Jamie Thomas]] || 10 || 31 || {{#expr:10/31 round 2}} || 2006–2011
|-
| 7 ||align=left| [[Myles Weston]] || 9 || 16 || {{#expr:9/16 round 2}} || 2014–2023
|-
| rowspan=3|8 ||align=left| [[Garfield Gonsalves]] || 8 || 29 || {{#expr:8/29 round 2}} || 1992–2008
|-
|align=left| [[Kerry Skepple]] || 8 || 34 || {{#expr:8/34 round 2}} || 2002–2012
|-
|align=left| [[Gayson Gregory]] || 8 || 49 || {{#expr:8/49 round 2}} || 2000–2014
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[FIFA World Cup]] record 
!rowspan=19|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record 
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad 
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]||colspan=9|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]||colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|4
|0
|0
|4
|3
|22
|-
|[[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] and [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]||colspan=9 rowspan=11|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|4
|1
|1
|2
|5
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|4
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea }} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|6
|1
|2
|3
|4
|15
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|4
|2
|0
|2
|7
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|12
|5
|1
|6
|32
|18
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|4
|2
|0
|2
|6
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|4
|2
|1
|1
|6
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|4
|0
|1
|3
|1
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]||colspan=9 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total!!—!!0/12!!colspan=7|—
!50!!15!!7!!28!!73!!103
|}

===CONCACAF Gold Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[CONCACAF Championship]] / [[CONCACAF Gold Cup|Gold Cup]] record
!rowspan=26|
!colspan=6|[[CONCACAF Gold Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad 
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1963 CONCACAF Championship|1963]] to [[1971 CONCACAF Championship|1971]]||colspan=9|''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|colspan=6|''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|- 
|{{flagicon|Haiti|1964}} [[1973 CONCACAF Championship|1973]]||colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|4	
|0	
|0	
|4	
|3	
|22
|- 
|[[1977 CONCACAF Championship|1977]] and [[1981 CONCACAF Championship|1981]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate'' 
|-
|[[File:Location North America.svg|20px]] [[1985 CONCACAF Championship|1985]]||colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|5
|-
|[[File:Location North America.svg|20px]] [[1989 CONCACAF Championship|1989]] 
|2
|1
|1
|0
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1991 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1991]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[1993 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1993]]||colspan=9 rowspan=17|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1	
|0
|1	
|1	
|1	
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]]
|3	
|1	
|2	
|2	
|6	
|11	
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1998]]
|2	
|0	
|0	
|2	
|1	
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2000 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2000]]
|11
|4
|2
|5
|24
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2002 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2002]]
|3	
|0	
|1	
|2	
|4	
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2003 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2003]]
|5  
|0  
|1   
|4   
|1  
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2005 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2005]]
|3	
|1	
|0	
|2	
|6	
|8	
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2007]]
|6 
|2   
|0  
|4   
|8   
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]]
|9  
|4    
|3   
|2   
|19 
|16
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]]
|6   
|2  
|2   
|2  
|4   
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2013 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2013]]
|3	
|1	
|0	
|2	
|3	
|3	
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2015 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2015]]
|9 
|4   
|2   
|3   
|14  
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2017 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2017]]
|6   
|3   
|0   
|3   
|10  
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Costa Rica}} {{flagicon|Jamaica}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]
|4
|2
|0
|2
|10
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]]
|6
|3
|0
|3
|8
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]
|7
|3
|0
|4
|5
|12
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2025]]
|6
|0
|1
|5
|2
|15
|-
!Total!!—!!0/20!!colspan=7|—
!99!!33!!15!!51!!132!!198
|}

===CONCACAF Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=22|[[CONCACAF Nations League]] record
|-
!colspan=11|League phase
!rowspan=8|
!colspan=10|Final phase
|-
!Season
!{{Tooltip|Div.|Division}}
!Group
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/Relegation}}
!Finals
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Squad|Finals Squad Only}}
|-
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League|2019−20]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group C|C]]
|7th||6||3||0||3||8||17||{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2021]]||colspan=9 rowspan=5|''Ineligible''
|-
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group A|A]]
|8th||6||3||0||3||5||7||{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2023]]
|-
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League|2023–24]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group D|D]]
|13th||6||1||1||4||9||22||{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2024 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2024]]
|-
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group D|D]]
|15th||6||0||1||5||2||15||{{fall}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2025]]
|-
|[[2026–27 CONCACAF Nations League|2026–27]]
|[[2026–27 CONCACAF Nations League C|C]]||colspan=9|''To be determined''
|[[2027 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2027]]
|-
!colspan=4|Total!!24!!7!!2!!15!!24!!61!!—
!Total!!colspan=9|—
|}
{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=2|CONCACAF Nations League history
|-
!First match
|{{fb|JAM}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|ATG}}<br>(6 September 2019; [[Montego Bay]], Jamaica)
|-
!Biggest win
|{{fb-rt|ATG}} 4–1 {{fb|BAH}}<br>(14 October 2023; [[Nassau, Bahamas|Nassau]], Bahamas)
|-
!Biggest defeat
|{{fb|JAM}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|ATG}}<br>(6 September 2019; Montego Bay, Jamaica)<br>{{fb|GUY}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|ATG}}<br>(21 November 2023; [[Santo Domingo]], Dominican Republic)
|-
!Best result
|7th – League B ([[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League#League B|2019–20]])
|-
!Worst result
|Relegation League C ([[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League#League B|2024–25]]) 
|}

===Caribbean Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[CFU Championship]] / [[Caribbean Cup]] record
|rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:#9acdff"
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1978 CFU Championship|1978]]
|'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''5'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''3'''||'''4'''||'''8'''
|2||1||1||0||3||0
|- 
|{{flagicon|SUR}} [[1979 CFU Championship|1979]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||0||2||0||2
|-
|{{flagicon|PUR|1952}} [[1981 CFU Championship|1981]]||colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate''
|- style="background:#9acdff"
|{{flagicon|GYF|local}} [[1983 CFU Championship|1983]]
|'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''7'''||'''2'''||'''2'''||'''3'''||'''11'''||'''10'''
|4||2||2||0||10||5
|-
|{{flagicon|BAR}} [[1985 CFU Championship|1985]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||1||0||1||2||2
|-bgcolor=silver
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[1988 CFU Championship|1988]]
| '''Runners-up'''||'''2nd'''||'''5'''||'''1'''||'''4'''||'''0'''||'''5'''||'''4'''
|2||1||1||0||2||1
|-
|{{flagicon|BRB}} [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|4||3||0||1||4||2
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1990 Caribbean Cup|1990]]
|3||0||1||2||2||5
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]]||colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]]
|Group stage||7th||3||0||1||2||2||12
|3||3||0||0||8||0	
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2||1||0||1||1||1	
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994]]
|2||1||0||1||10||3
|- 
|{{flagicon|CAY}} {{flagicon|JAM}} [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]
|Group stage||7th||3||1||0||2||3||8
|2||0||2||0||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|- 
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|ATG}} {{flagicon|SKN}} [[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997]]
|Group stage||6th||2||0||0||2||1||5
|—
|colspan=6|''Qualified as hosts''
|- style="background:#9acdff"
|{{flagicon|JAM}} {{flagicon|TRI}} [[1998 Caribbean Cup|1998]]
|'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''5'''||'''2'''||'''0'''||'''3'''||'''11'''||'''9'''
|3||2||1||0||11||3
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1999 Caribbean Cup|1999]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|3||0||1||2||2||5
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2001 Caribbean Cup|2001]]
|3||0||1||2||4||5
|-
|{{flagicon|BRB}} [[2005 Caribbean Cup|2005]]
|3||1||0||2||6||8
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007]]
|6||2||0||4||8||17
|- 
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]
|Group stage||7th||3||0||2||1||3||6
|6||4||1||1||16||10
|- 
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2010 Caribbean Cup|2010]]
|Group stage||5th||3||1||0||2||2||4
|3||1||2||0||2||1	
|- 
|style="border: 3px solid red" |{{flagicon|ATG}} [[2012 Caribbean Cup|2012]]
|Group stage||8th||3||1||0||2||3||3
|colspan=6|''Qualified as hosts''
|- 
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2014 Caribbean Cup|2014]]
|Group stage||7th||3||0||1||2||2||7
|6||4||1||1||12||4
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2017 Caribbean Cup|2017]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|6||3||0||3||10||8
|-
!Total!!Runners-up!!11/22!!42!!9!!11!!22!!47!!76
!67!!30!!14!!23!!116!!85
|}

===Central American and Caribbean Games===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Central American and Caribbean Games|Central American and Caribbean Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Pos.
!Pld
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|[[Football at the 1930 Central American and Caribbean Games|1930]] to [[Football at the 1982 Central American and Caribbean Games|1982]]||colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|DOM}} [[Football at the 1986 Central American and Caribbean Games|1986]]
|Quarter-finals||5th||2||0||1||1||1||2
|-
!Total!!Quarter-finals!!1/1!!2!!0!!1!!1!!1!!2
|}

==Head-to-head record==
The following table shows Antigua and Barbuda's all-time official international record per opponent:<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.11v11.com/teams/antigua-and-barbuda/tab/stats|title=Antigua And Barbuda national football team statistics and records: All-time record|publisher=11v11.com|access-date=22 November 2014|archive-date=29 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141129073051/http://www.11v11.com/teams/antigua-and-barbuda/tab/stats|url-status=live}}</ref>
<br>''Updated to 18 November 2025 after the match against [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]]''
;Key
{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Winning record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Equal record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Losing record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

{| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:100%;"
|-
! Opponent
! {{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
! {{Tooltip|W|Won}}
! {{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
! {{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
! {{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
! {{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|AIA}} || 5 || 5 || 0 || 0 || 28 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|ARU}} || 6 || 5 || 1 || 0 || 11 || 4
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BAH}} || 3 || 2 || 1 || 0 || 12 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BRB}} || 8 || 1 || 1 || 6 || 9 || 16
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BER}} || 14 || 3 || 5 || 6 || 14 || 18
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|VGB}} || 8 || 4 || 2 || 2 || 14 || 10
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CAY}} || 3 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 1 || 4
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CUB}} || 8 || 0 || 1 || 7 || 4 || 19
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CUW}} || 4 || 3 || 0 || 1 || 10 || 6
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|DMA}} || 17 || 8 || 5 || 4 || 28 || 20
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|DOM}} || 8 || 1 || 2 || 5 || 8 || 21
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SLV}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|EST}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GUF}} || 1 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GRN}} || 6 || 3 || 3 || 0 || 13 || 7
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GLP}}|| 7 || 2 || 1 || 4 || 4 || 12
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GUA}} || 8 || 1 || 0 || 7 || 3 || 31
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GUY}} || 15 || 5 || 3 || 7 || 17 || 37
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|HAI}} || 12 || 2 || 3 || 7 || 11 || 19
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|HON}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|HUN}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|JAM}} || 12 || 1 || 2 || 9 || 5 || 29
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|MTQ}} || 4 || 1 || 0 || 3 || 6 || 12
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|MEX}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 8
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|MSR}} || 4 || 3 || 1 || 0 || 20 || 6
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|PUR}} || 4 || 1 || 0 || 3 || 5 || 10
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SKN}} || 23 || 9 || 5 || 9 || 30 || 31
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|LCA}} || 14 || 5 || 3 || 6 || 23 || 18
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SVG}} || 13 || 7 || 2 || 4 || 17 || 20
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SXM}} || 3 || 2 || 0 || 1 || 7 || 2
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SUR}} || 6 || 1 || 2 || 3 || 4 || 15
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|TRI}} || 13 || 2 || 0 || 11 || 12 || 41
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|VIR}} || 5 || 5 || 0 || 0 || 26 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|USA}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 2 || 5
|-class="sortbottom"
! Total || 240 || 83 || 43 || 114 || 342 || 428
|}

==Honours==
===Subregional===
* '''[[CFU Championship]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[1988 CFU Championship|1988]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[Leeward Islands Tournament]] (2)''': 1949, 1994

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{commons category}}
* {{Official website|http://www.antiguafootball.com/}}

{{Antigua and Barbuda national football team}}
{{Football in Antigua and Barbuda}}
{{CONCACAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Antigua and Barbuda}}

[[Category:Antigua and Barbuda national football team| ]]
[[Category:Caribbean national association football teams]]
[[Category:Men's national sports teams of Antigua and Barbuda|Football]]
